campaign: v0.13 validation campaign - CPU lanes A4-A7
note_on_xla_warnings: A5/A7 logs contain thousands of benign XLA:CPU AOT 'machine feature +prefer-no-gather/+prefer-no-scatter not supported on host' warnings from a persistent JIT cache compiled on a different CPU feature set. They are non-fatal (the runtime recompiles) and A7's PASS confirms they do not affect correctness; they are NOT the A5 failure cause.

lanes.name: A4
lanes.title: Community dycore / conservation / restart gate
lanes.command: taskset -c 12-23 env JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu PYTHONPATH=src bash scripts/community_validation.sh

key_numbers.straka_density_current: RAN_TO_COMPLETION PASS, front position 14150 m (target 15000 +/- 2000), max|w|=14.58 m/s, theta' min=-9.97 K, rel mass drift 2.25e-09, wall 176.6 s
key_numbers.skamarock_warm_bubble: RAN_TO_COMPLETION PASS, thermal rise 1924 m, theta' max=1.92 K (0.5-2.5), max|w|=11.68 m/s, rel mass drift 0.0, wall 19.0 s
key_numbers.closed_domain_conservation: PASS, dry-mass rel residual 0.0, total-water rel residual -2.45e-16, MSE residual 0.0 J (tol dry 1e-10 / water 1e-08)
key_numbers.bitwise_restart: PASS, full-carry bit-identical=true, stochastic-seed bit-identical=true, 203-var wrfrst NetCDF round-trip

lanes.proves: Cheap analytic + idealized + conservation + structural-restart gates remain green on the v0.13 tip.

lanes.name: A5
lanes.title: Operational physics-suite coupler smoke
lanes.command: taskset -c 12-15 env JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu JAX_ENABLE_X64=true PYTHONPATH=src python proofs/v060/multicfg_operational_smoke.py --steps 8 --out $OUT/a5_multicfg_operational_smoke.json

failure.crashes_on: first SWEEP config (no scheme exercised at all)

lanes.root_cause: v0.13 added 'ra_sw_physics' and 'ra_lw_physics' to _SCAN_WIRED_OPTIONS (operational_mode.py:2431,2435). _resolve_operational_suite reads every wired key via getattr WITHOUT a default. The proof harness stub _NLStub.__init__ (proofs/v060/multicfg_operational_smoke.py:298-314) was NOT updated to set ra_sw_physics/ra_lw_physics, so it raises AttributeError.
lanes.scope: PROOF-HARNESS GAP, NOT a production regression. The real OperationalNamelist dataclass (operational_mode.py:393) defines ra_sw_physics:int=4 and ra_lw_physics with defaults, so the public run_forecast_operational path is unaffected. Only the multicfg smoke stub is stale.
lanes.fix_hint: Add self.ra_sw_physics = getattr(cfg,'ra_sw_physics',4) and self.ra_lw_physics = getattr(cfg,'ra_lw_physics',4) (or pull from cfg.as_namelist()) to _NLStub.__init__. Then A5 should run the full sweep.
lanes.honest_fail_closed_assessment: CANNOT be assessed this run: the smoke never reached its per-config accept/reject logic. Coverage (Thompson/WSM6/WDM6/Morrison/Kessler/Lin/WSM3/WSM5; MYNN/MYJ/YSU/ACM2/BouLac/MRF; sfclayrev/MYNN/Janjic/GFS/old-MM5; Noah-MP/Noah-classic; KF/BMJ/GF/Tiedtke; RRTMG SW/LW) is UNVERIFIED. This becomes an immediate debug lane.
lanes.proves: NOTHING this run - the operational coupler smoke is blocked by a stale stub on the v0.13 tip.

lanes.name: A6
lanes.title: v0.13 focused operational wiring pytest
lanes.command: taskset -c 16-19 env JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu JAX_ENABLE_X64=true PYTHONPATH=src python -m pytest -q tests/test_v013_myj_janjic_operational.py tests/test_v013_mrf_operational.py tests/test_v013_t3_surface_lsm_wiring.py tests/test_v013_ra_sw_gsfc.py tests/test_v060_ra_sw_dudhia.py tests/test_rrtm_lw_operational_wiring.py tests/test_gwd_operational_wiring.py tests/test_v0110_boundary_feedback.py tests/test_p0_1a_nesting.py
lanes.in_flight_note: No failure observed in test_v013_mrf_operational.py or test_v013_t3_surface_lsm_wiring.py; the parallel MRF/surface wiring change (if any) did not break these tests on this tip.
lanes.proves: MYJ/Janjic, MRF, GFS/old-MM5, GSFC SW, Dudhia SW, RRTM LW, GWD, nesting and 2-way feedback wiring are all green.

lanes.name: A7
lanes.title: Reference / fail-closed / fake-mesh oracle sweep
lanes.command: for each of proofs/v013/{t3_microphysics_oracle,myj_janjic_oracle,mrf_oracle,t3_surface_lsm_oracle,t3_radiation_oracle,t3_cumulus_oracle}.py: taskset -c 20-23 env JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu JAX_ENABLE_X64=true PYTHONPATH=src python <script>; plus fakemesh with XLA_FLAGS=--xla_force_host_platform_device_count=8

key_numbers.microphysics: WSM7 oracle parity PASS; default mp byte-unchanged PASS; fail-closed PASS; OVERALL PASS
key_numbers.surface_lsm: verdict PASS; slab_lsm worst |jax-oracle|=2.33e-10, old_mm5_sfclay 2.27e-12, gfs_sfclay 2.16e-12
key_numbers.radiation: OVERALL PASS vs gfortran pristine-WRF oracle; held-rate RTH rel err <= 1.26e-04, GSW err <= 8.3e-04; FP64 audit RTH rel <= 1.3e-07
key_numbers.cumulus: GATE_PASS; operational cu=[0,1,2,3,6] byte-unchanged; New-Tiedtke(16) + KIM-SAS(14) honestly 'oracle-staged (kernel carry-over)'; all_fail_closed_ok=True
key_numbers.fakemesh: gate_pass=True; partition-invariant bit-identity for P=2,4,8 vs P=1 (flux5 advection + 6th-order diffusion bit-identical); padded_eager==global_eager exact; honestly labeled CPU fake-device only, multi-GPU throughput PROJECTED not MEASURED

lanes.proves: Reference/fail-closed side of the implemented suite is not drifting and is honestly labeled; multi-GPU decomposition math is deterministic + partition-invariant on a fake 8-device mesh, with no false real-multi-GPU throughput claim.

summary.A5: FAIL (proof-harness stub gap, not production regression)
summary.biggest_reassurance: A6 (89/89 operational-wiring tests) + A7 (all 6 oracles PASS vs gfortran pristine-WRF + fakemesh partition-invariant bit-identity) + A4 (idealized dycore + conservation + restart all green): the v0.13 implemented couplings, reference/fail-closed gates, and decomposition math are sound on the trunk tip.
summary.biggest_concern: A5 operational coupler smoke cannot run on the v0.13 tip: the multicfg smoke's _NLStub is stale (missing ra_sw_physics/ra_lw_physics that v0.13 added to _SCAN_WIRED_OPTIONS). The full operational-coupler scheme matrix (all MP/PBL/SFC/LSM/CU/RA families through the device scan) is therefore UNVERIFIED end-to-end this run. Production path is unaffected, but the smoke must be repaired to re-establish that gate.
